title: Ainsliaea silhetensis
intro: Ainsliaea silhetensis is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as a flowering plant native to the Himalayas. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Ainsliaea silhetensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ainsliaea silhetensis is commonly used to prepare herbal formulations such as decoctions, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory infections and skin disorders.
